headlines.title: Seattle Seahawks Complete 2026 NFL Draft with Eight-Player Class
headlines.summary: The Seattle Seahawks wrapped up their 2026 NFL Draft class on Saturday, April 25, selecting eight players including Kansas wide receiver Emmanuel Henderson Jr. in the sixth round and Arizona cornerback Michael in the seventh. The team focused on smaller, quicker pass rushers and added depth at cornerback and wide receiver.

headlines.title: Man Sentenced to 3.5 Years for Shooting Disabled Veteran on Seattle Waterfront
headlines.summary: Gregory Timm was sentenced to three and a half years in prison for the July 2025 shooting of Harold Powell Sr., a wheelchair-bound Navy veteran and street performer, on Seattle's waterfront during Seafair. The victim offered forgiveness during the sentencing hearing.

headlines.title: Southwest Airlines Launches 'Sip and Ship' Program in Seattle
headlines.summary: Starting April 24, Southwest Airlines passengers flying from Seattle and other select West Coast cities can check one case of wine (up to 12 bottles) at no cost. The new 'Sip and Ship' program allows travelers to bring home wine from wine country destinations without checked baggage fees, saving $45 per passenger.

headlines.title: Adams Elementary Parents Confront Seattle School Board Over Principal Appointment
headlines.summary: Parents packed the Seattle School Board meeting to express concerns about the appointment of Principal Anitra Jones at Adams Elementary in Ballard. Parents raised issues about the selection process and Jones' prior tenure at Rainier View Elementary, citing concerns about teacher retention and leadership.

headlines.title: Fireball Spotted Over Western Washington
headlines.summary: A bright fireball was spotted streaking across the sky over western Washington. According to reports, the meteor was first seen about 50 miles above Wilkeson, Washington, traveling northwest at approximately 39,000 mph before disintegrating 30 miles above Wauna on the north shore of Carr Inlet.

headlines.title: Pierce County Cold Case from 1992 Leads to Arrest
headlines.summary: A 33-year-old cold case in Pierce County has led to an arrest. The husband of a Graham woman who disappeared in 1992 was taken into custody in connection with her death, bringing new developments to one of the region's longest-running unsolved cases.

headlines.title: Chateau Ste Michelle Unveils 2026 Summer Concert Lineup
headlines.summary: Woodinville's Chateau Ste Michelle winery has announced its 2026 summer concert series lineup. The popular outdoor venue will host performances from various artists including Bob Dylan scheduled for June 6, continuing the winery's tradition of combining wine and live music in a scenic setting.

headlines.title: Dog's Ashes and Memorial Items Stolen from Car in Seattle's U District
headlines.summary: A theft in Seattle's University District has left a pet owner devastated after a dog's ashes and memorial items were stolen from a parked car. The incident highlights ongoing concerns about vehicle break-ins in the neighborhood.
